name: Tulsi
preview: Tulsi (Ocimum sanctum) is known as the 'Queen of Herbs' in Ayurveda. It boosts immunity, combats respiratory disorders, and has adaptogenic properties.

name: Amla
preview: Amla (Emblica officinalis) is a potent source of Vitamin C. It rejuvenates tissues, improves digestion, eyesight, and balances all three doshas.

name: Ashwagandha
preview: Ashwagandha (Withania somnifera) is a powerful adaptogen. It reduces stress, increases strength, and supports reproductive and nervous systems.

name: Giloy
preview: Giloy (Tinospora cordifolia) is a powerful immunity booster. It acts as an antipyretic, detoxifier, and rejuvenator with anti-inflammatory effects.

name: Shatavari
preview: Shatavari (Asparagus racemosus) supports female reproductive health, boosts vitality, and is a top Ayurvedic rejuvenator for Pitta and Vata.

name: Nagarmotha
preview: Used to treat indigestion, fever, and obesity through its deepana and pachana actions.

name: Agnimantha
preview: Part of Dashamoola, used in vata disorders and for promoting digestion.

name: Rohitaka
preview: Used for treating liver disorders, ascites, and splenomegaly.

name: Chopchini
preview: Used in treating joint disorders and skin diseases, known for its anti-inflammatory action.

name: Bhringraj
preview: Promotes hair growth, supports liver function, and has rejuvenating qualities.

name: Jatamansi
preview: Calms the mind, supports mental clarity, and acts as a nervine tonic.

name: Chandana
preview: Cooling herb used in Pitta disorders, urinary tract infections, and skin diseases.

name: Kutaj
preview: Treats diarrhea, dysentery and balances kapha and pitta dosha.

name: Rasona (Garlic)
preview: Used for respiratory, digestive, and circulatory disorders. Known for lekhana and deepana properties.

name: Kanchanar
preview: Kanchanar is used to reduce glandular swellings, support thyroid, and treat goiter.

name: Karpura (Camphor)
preview: Camphor has stimulant, antispasmodic, and antiseptic actions, used in respiratory and skin conditions.

name: Pushkarmool
preview: Effective for asthma, cough, and dyspnea. Acts as a bronchodilator and expectorant.

name: Bakuchi
preview: Widely used in leucoderma, vitiligo, and other skin disorders. Supports melanin production.

name: Vidarikand
preview: Acts as a nutritive tonic, improves fertility, and strengthens the body.

name: Ajmoda
preview: Improves digestion, relieves bloating and abdominal discomfort, and acts as carminative.

name: Patola
preview: Used in fever, liver disorders, and skin diseases. Strong pitta pacifier.

name: Vrikshamla
preview: Helps in weight management and digestion. Rich in hydroxycitric acid.

name: Kumuda
preview: Useful in burning sensation, pitta disorders and fever.

name: Shigru
preview: Moringa is rich in nutrients, detoxifies blood, and improves digestion.

name: Parpataka
preview: Reduces burning sensation, used in fever, acidity and liver ailments.

name: Indrayava
preview: Treats diarrhea, dysentery and helps regulate bowel movements.

name: Kapikachhu
preview: Improves fertility, nervous strength and acts as aphrodisiac.

name: Bala
preview: Improves strength and stamina, useful in vata disorders and nervous weakness.

name: Tamalpatra
preview: Bay leaf improves appetite, respiration and balances kapha dosha.

name: Chitrak
preview: Ignites digestive fire, used in weight loss and ama digestion.

name: Tagar
preview: Relieves stress, induces sleep and calms the nervous system.

name: Khadira
preview: Purifies blood, useful in skin diseases, leprosy, and itching.

name: Twak (Cinnamon)
preview: Stimulates digestion, circulation and balances kapha dosha.

name: Ela
preview: Improves digestion, relieves nausea and freshens breath.

name: Sariva
preview: Sariva is known for its blood purifying and cooling properties, useful in skin disorders.

name: Gorakhmundi
preview: Used in urinary tract infections, liver disorders, and as a detoxifying herb.

name: Kokilaksha
preview: A nerve tonic and aphrodisiac, used for urinary and reproductive health.

name: Yavani
preview: Relieves abdominal pain, bloating and indigestion; acts as carminative.

name: Bhunimba
preview: Bhunimba is a bitter herb helpful in fever, liver disorders, and pitta conditions.

name: Chavya
preview: Promotes digestion and clears ama; excellent for vata and kapha conditions.

name: Palasha
preview: Known for its anti-helminthic properties, Palasha helps eliminate intestinal parasites.

name: Karanja
preview: Used in skin disorders, leprosy, and inflammation due to its detoxifying nature.

name: Ahiphena
preview: Processed opium used in Ayurveda for severe pain and diarrhea management under supervision.

name: Danti
preview: Strong purgative used in detox and management of constipation and ascites.

name: Eranda
preview: Castor plant used for joint pain, digestive issues and vata-related disorders.

name: Guggulu
preview: Excellent for arthritis, high cholesterol, and detoxification.

name: Bhallataka
preview: Powerful digestive and stimulant, used under medical supervision for vata disorders.

name: Vasaka
preview: Effective in bronchitis, asthma and respiratory disorders.

name: Laksha
preview: Used in bone healing, fractures, and skin diseases.

name: Talisapatra
preview: Useful in cough, asthma, cold and sore throat.

name: Tamalaki
preview: Effective in liver diseases, hepatitis, jaundice and digestion.

name: Shirisha
preview: Main anti-allergic herb; used in poisoning and respiratory issues.

name: Babbula
preview: Effective in dental issues, bleeding gums, and skin ailments.

name: Kumkuma
preview: Saffron used for complexion, mood elevation, and female fertility.

name: Bilva
preview: Bilva is useful in digestive disorders, diarrhea, and IBS due to its binding effect.

name: Ashoka
preview: A prime uterine tonic, it regulates menstruation and reduces cramps.

name: Devadaru
preview: Useful in cough, cold and joint pain due to its anti-inflammatory action.

name: Patala
preview: Used in Dashamoola formulation, helps in vata disorders and pain relief.

name: Dhataki
preview: Flowers used in fermentation, female health and diarrhea treatment.

name: Yava
preview: Barley grain improves digestion, is diuretic and ideal in obesity.

name: Masha
preview: Black gram nourishes muscles, increases strength and semen.

name: Chana
preview: Bengal gram is nourishing and helps in muscle building.

name: Matulunga
preview: Citrus lemon balances vata, aids digestion and appetite.

name: Dadima
preview: Pomegranate enhances appetite and relieves burning sensation.

name: Tinduka
preview: Used in dysentery, ulcers, and excessive menstruation.

name: Draksha
preview: Grapes are nourishing and relieve thirst, fatigue, and constipation.

name: Chakramarda
preview: Effective in skin disorders, ringworm, and constipation.

name: Pashanabheda
preview: Effective in kidney stones, urinary infections, and painful urination.

name: Lodhra
preview: Beneficial in gynecological issues, acne, and bleeding disorders.

name: Jati
preview: Useful for joint pain, inflammation and respiratory conditions.

name: Shalparni
preview: Part of Dashamoola, balances Vata, relieves fatigue and strengthens body.

name: Kantakari
preview: Effective in asthma, cough, and respiratory diseases.

name: Gambhari
preview: Used in Dashamoola; helps with inflammation, pain, and Vata disorders.

name: Gojihva
preview: Cools the body, improves immunity and reduces burning sensation.

name: Aparajita
preview: Used for skin diseases, swelling, and mental rejuvenation.

name: Meshashringi
preview: Known for regulating blood sugar and metabolism.

name: Ikshu
preview: Sugarcane improves energy, acts as diuretic and cooling.

name: Amrita
preview: Divine herb for immunity, detox and chronic fevers.

name: Shigru Patra
preview: Drumstick leaves improve nutrition, blood, and joint health.

name: Haridra
preview: Turmeric is powerful anti-inflammatory and detoxifying herb.

name: Mandukparni
preview: Supports memory, intellect and improves mental function.

name: Vatsanabha
preview: Processed aconite, used in fevers, pain and digestion.

name: Ushira
preview: Vetiver root is cooling, soothing and diuretic.

name: Kiratatikta
preview: Bitter tonic used for fever, appetite, and digestion.

name: Shilajit
preview: Famous rejuvenator and energy booster for all ages.

name: Kusha
preview: Sacred grass used for purification and urinary issues.

name: Bimbi
preview: Used in diabetes, obesity, and blood sugar regulation.

name: Nirgundi
preview: Reduces pain, swelling, and Vata-related disorders.

name: Tagetes
preview: Marigold used in skin, fever, and pitta disorders.

name: Kachura
preview: Aromatic rhizome for respiratory and digestive issues.

name: Shati
preview: Enhances respiratory strength and prevents infections.

name: Bhanga
preview: Cannabis leaf used in pain, digestion, and sleep disorders.

name: Mochras
preview: Cooling resin used in bleeding and Pitta disorders.

name: Jivanti
preview: Nourishing herb for lactation and immunity.

name: Makoy
preview: Solanum nigrum for liver and spleen disorders.

name: Kampillaka
preview: Used for skin problems and worm infestations.

name: Amlavetasa
preview: Used in dyspepsia, obesity and to stimulate digestion.

name: Ardraka
preview: Fresh ginger root used for digestion, nausea and cold.

name: Parijata
preview: Night jasmine, good for cough, fever and arthritis.

name: Rajika
preview: Mustard seeds help in digestion and pain relief.

name: Rakta Chandana
preview: Red sandalwood for skin, bleeding disorders and cooling.

name: Surana
preview: Elephant foot yam used for piles, swelling and digestion.

name: Vasa
preview: Adhatoda vasica for cough, bleeding and Pitta conditions.

name: Lajjalu
preview: Touch-me-not plant for bleeding piles and wounds.

name: Manjistha
preview: Powerful blood purifier for skin and liver.

name: Atibala
preview: Used in urinary and nervous disorders, strengthens the body.

name: Karvellaka
preview: Bitter gourd used in diabetes and liver disorders.

name: Katuki
preview: Used in liver cleansing, detox, and skin conditions.

name: Yastimadhu
preview: Licorice used for cough, acidity, and skin glow.

name: Haritaki
preview: A key component of Triphala, Haritaki is a powerful rejuvenator and detoxifier, especially for Vata dosha. It supports digestion and colon health.

name: Bibhitaki
preview: The second fruit of Triphala, Bibhitaki is excellent for balancing Kapha dosha. It supports the respiratory system and purifies the blood.

name: Pippali
preview: Long pepper is a powerful stimulant for the digestive and respiratory systems. It is a key ingredient in the Trikatu formula.

name: Maricha
preview: Black pepper enhances digestion, stimulates metabolism, and helps clear congestion. It is a part of the Trikatu formula.

name: Shunthi
preview: Dry ginger is known as the 'universal medicine'. It aids digestion, reduces inflammation, and pacifies Vata and Kapha.

name: Brahmi
preview: Brahmi (Bacopa monnieri) is a premier herb for the brain and nervous system, enhancing memory, concentration, and calming the mind.

name: Vidanga
preview: False black pepper is the primary Ayurvedic herb for eliminating intestinal worms and parasites.

name: Arjuna
preview: Arjuna is the foremost cardiotonic in Ayurveda, strengthening the heart muscle and supporting overall cardiovascular health.

name: Punarnava
preview: Known for its diuretic and rejuvenating properties, Punarnava supports kidney and liver function and reduces swelling.

name: Vacha
preview: Sweet flag is a powerful herb for the mind, improving speech, intelligence, and clearing toxins from the nervous system.

name: Hingu
preview: Asafoetida is a potent digestive stimulant that alleviates gas, bloating, and abdominal pain. Excellent for Vata dosha.

name: Jeeraka
preview: Cumin seeds are a superb digestive aid, improving absorption and relieving gas and cramps.

name: Dhanyaka
preview: Coriander is a cooling digestive herb that soothes an irritated gut, relieves thirst, and pacifies Pitta dosha.

name: Methi
preview: Fenugreek seeds are used to balance blood sugar, improve digestion, and support lactation.

name: Shallaki
preview: Boswellia serrata is a powerful anti-inflammatory herb used to support joint health and relieve arthritic pain.

name: Jyotishmati
preview: Known as the 'intellect tree', its oil is used to sharpen memory and improve cognitive function.

name: Japa
preview: Hibiscus flower is used to purify blood, support hair health, and cool the body, making it excellent for Pitta.

name: Kamala
preview: The sacred Lotus has cooling properties and is used in bleeding disorders and to calm the mind and heart.

name: Ketaki
preview: Kewra flower is used in Ayurveda for its aromatic properties and to support brain and nerve health.

name: Atasi
preview: Flax seeds are a rich source of healthy fats, used to balance Vata, lubricate the intestines, and support skin health.

name: Tila
preview: Sesame seeds and their oil are profoundly nourishing, especially for Vata dosha, supporting bone, skin, and hair health.

name: Brihati
preview: A member of the Dashamoola, Brihati is used to support respiratory health and is effective in treating cough and cold.

name: Gokshura
preview: Tribulus terrestris is a renowned tonic for the urinary system, supporting kidney and bladder health, and acts as an aphrodisiac.

name: Hamsapadi
preview: A lesser-known but potent herb used for blood purification and as a tonic for the nervous system.

name: Lavanga
preview: Clove is a powerful aromatic herb that acts as a pain reliever, especially for toothaches, and stimulates digestion.

name: Nagakesara
preview: Used primarily in bleeding disorders due to its astringent and hemostatic properties. It is also good for the skin.

name: Sarpagandha
preview: Indian snakeroot is a powerful herb for managing high blood pressure and calming anxiety, but should be used with caution.

name: Varuna
preview: Varuna is a specific herb for urinary health, known for its ability to break down kidney stones and support prostate health.

name: Aragwadha
preview: Known as Cassia fistula, Aragwadha is a potent but gentle laxative, excellent for clearing Pitta and ama from the digestive tract.

name: Apamarga
preview: Prickly Chaff Flower is used for its cleansing properties, especially in panchakarma. It aids in digestion and detoxification.

name: Trivrit
preview: Operculina turpethum is a strong purgative herb used in Ayurveda for detoxification and relieving constipation.

name: Durva
preview: Bermuda grass is a revered cooling herb that purifies the blood and stops bleeding, excellent for Pitta conditions.

name: Jatiphala
preview: Nutmeg is an aromatic stimulant used for improving digestion, inducing sleep, and relieving pain.

name: Kulattha
preview: Horse gram is used for treating kidney stones, cough, and asthma. It has a heating and Kapha-reducing effect.

name: Amra
preview: Mango (ripe) is nourishing and strength-promoting, while the unripe fruit and seed are astringent and used for diarrhea.

name: Jambu
preview: Jamun or Black Plum is used in managing diabetes and diarrhea due to its astringent and blood sugar-regulating properties.

name: Snuhi
preview: Euphorbia neriifolia is a very potent purgative herb used under expert guidance for severe constipation and skin diseases.

name: Kasani
preview: Chicory is a well-known liver tonic that supports detoxification, digestion, and urinary health.

name: Sharapunkha
preview: Tephrosia purpurea is a renowned herb for liver and spleen disorders and also acts as a blood purifier.

name: Prishniparni
preview: A member of the Dashamoola group, Uraria picta is a tonic that balances all three doshas and supports fracture healing.

name: Jayapala
preview: Croton tiglium is the most potent purgative in Ayurveda and must be used only after purification and in minute doses.

name: Gunja
preview: Abrus precatorius seeds are toxic but used after purification for skin diseases and as a nervine tonic.

name: Karavira
preview: Oleander is a toxic plant used externally after processing for skin diseases and wound healing.

name: Kadamba
preview: Neolamarckia cadamba is a cooling herb used to pacify Pitta, manage fever, and treat ulcers.

name: Badara
preview: Jujube fruit (Ber) is nourishing and acts as a mild laxative, while its leaves are used for wound healing.

name: Kapittha
preview: Wood Apple is used to treat chronic diarrhea and dysentery; the unripe fruit is a powerful astringent.

name: Kharjura
preview: Dates are highly nourishing, strength-promoting, and act as a natural aphrodisiac and tonic.

name: Langali
preview: Gloriosa superba is a potent herb used for inducing labor and in treating skin diseases, but is toxic if not used correctly.

name: Kankola
preview: Cubeb pepper is a diuretic and antiseptic, useful in urinary tract infections and respiratory congestion.

name: Mudga
preview: Green Gram (Mung Bean) is considered the best among pulses. It is light, easy to digest, and balancing for all doshas.

name: Masura
preview: Lentil is astringent and light, useful in managing diarrhea and bleeding disorders.

name: Ishwari
preview: Aristolochia indica is a potent anti-venom and anti-inflammatory herb, used for snake bites and fevers.

name: Priyangu
preview: Callicarpa macrophylla is a cooling herb used for skin diseases, bleeding disorders, and headache.

name: Dhattura
preview: A toxic plant (Jimsonweed) used in purified form for asthma, pain, and skin diseases.

name: Ativisha
preview: Aconitum heterophyllum is a key herb for fever, diarrhea, and cough, especially in children.

name: Vamsha
preview: Bamboo manna (Vanshlochan) is used as a tonic for the respiratory system, especially in cough and cold.

name: Palandu
preview: Onion is a Vata-pacifying vegetable, acting as an aphrodisiac and nervine tonic, but can aggravate Pitta.

name: Trapusha
preview: Cucumber is a cooling diuretic, excellent for pacifying Pitta, quenching thirst, and urinary health.

name: Hrivera
preview: Also known as Valaka, this herb is used for fever, burning sensation, and digestive issues like diarrhea.

name: Plaksha
preview: A species of Fig tree, its bark is used for bleeding disorders, ulcers, and to pacify Pitta and Kapha.

name: Udumbara
preview: Gular Fig is a cooling, astringent herb used in the treatment of bleeding disorders and diabetes.

name: Ashwattha
preview: The sacred Peepal tree's bark is used for its cooling and astringent properties, healing wounds and purifying blood.

name: Puga
preview: Areca nut (Betel nut) is a stimulant and astringent, but is used cautiously in Ayurveda.

name: Kakamachi
preview: Black nightshade is a tridoshic herb used for skin diseases, liver disorders, and as a rejuvenative.

name: Mishreya
preview: Fennel seeds (Saunf) are excellent for digestion, relieving gas, and have a cooling effect on the body.

name: Shankhpushpi
preview: A premier brain tonic (Medhya rasayana), Shankhpushpi is renowned for enhancing memory, intellect, and calming the mind.

name: Kushtha
preview: Saussurea lappa is a strong Vata-pacifying herb, used for pain, respiratory ailments, and skin diseases.

name: Madanaphala
preview: Known as Emetic Nut, it is the best herb for inducing therapeutic vomiting (Vamana) in Panchakarma.

name: Patha
preview: Cissampelos pareira is a potent herb for detoxification, treating fever, urinary disorders, and diarrhea.

name: Saptaparna
preview: Alstonia scholaris is a bitter tonic used primarily for skin diseases, intestinal worms, and malarial fever.

name: Prasarini
preview: Paederia foetida is a specific remedy for neurological disorders with pain, especially arthritis and sciatica.

name: Asana
preview: Pterocarpus marsupium is a well-known anti-diabetic herb that also purifies the blood and helps with skin issues.

name: Kushmanda
preview: Ash Gourd is a highly cooling and nourishing vegetable, acting as a diuretic and nervine tonic.

name: Vartaku
preview: Brinjal or Eggplant stimulates digestion and appetite but can aggravate Pitta if consumed in excess.

name: Dronapushpi
preview: Leucas cephalotes is used for fever, jaundice, and inflammatory conditions, especially liver-related issues.

name: Swarnapatri
preview: Known as Senna, it is a strong stimulant laxative used for acute constipation.

name: Chandrashura
preview: Lepidium sativum seeds are highly nutritive, promoting strength and lactation after delivery.

name: Chirabilva
preview: Holoptelea integrifolia is used in digestive disorders and to treat edema and piles.

name: Himsra
preview: Capparis sepiaria is a well-known hepatoprotective herb, commonly found in liver-support formulas.

name: Karkatashringi
preview: Pistacia integerrima galls are used in treating cough, asthma, and diarrhea, especially in children.

name: Katphala
preview: Myrica esculenta bark is used for respiratory ailments like cough, cold, and fever.

name: Krishna Jeeraka
preview: Black Cumin (Carum carvi) is a powerful digestive and carminative, stronger than regular cumin.

name: Murva
preview: Marsdenia tenacissima is a bitter tonic used for fever, skin diseases and purifying blood.

name: Paribhadra
preview: Erythrina variegata is used for its anti-inflammatory and nervine sedative properties, especially in Vata disorders.

name: Shyonaka
preview: A key ingredient of Dashamoola, Oroxylum indicum is an astringent and anti-inflammatory used in diarrhea and arthritis.

name: Alabu
preview: Bottle Gourd is a cooling, calming vegetable that is very easy to digest, making it ideal for convalescence.

name: Vriddhadaru
preview: Argyreia nervosa is a potent rejuvenative and aphrodisiac, enhancing strength and vitality.

name: Narikela
preview: Coconut is highly nourishing, cooling, and strength-promoting, pacifying Vata and Pitta.

name: Talamuli
preview: Curculigo orchioides is an aphrodisiac and uterine tonic, similar in properties to Shatavari.

name: Saireyaka
preview: Barleria prionitis is used for skin diseases, swelling, and oral health issues like bleeding gums.

name: Varahikanda
preview: Dioscorea bulbifera is a rejuvenative tonic (rasayana) that enhances strength, immunity, and acts as an aphrodisiac.

name: Ankola
preview: Alangium salviifolium is used in Ayurveda for its anti-venom properties and in treating skin diseases.

name: Changeri
preview: Indian Sorrel is used for digestive ailments like IBS and malabsorption due to its sour taste and digestive properties.

name: Koshataki
preview: Luffa is a bitter vegetable used to cleanse the liver and purify the blood.

name: Tanduliyaka
preview: Amaranth is a cooling herb used to treat toxicity, bleeding disorders, and purify the blood.

name: Ingudi
preview: Balanites roxburghii is used for its anti-microbial and anti-inflammatory properties, especially in skin diseases.

name: Jivaka
preview: One of the eight 'Ashtavarga' herbs, Jivaka is a vital rejuvenative tonic, often substituted with other herbs today.

name: Rishabhaka
preview: Another Ashtavarga herb, Rishabhaka is a powerful tonic for building tissues and promoting vitality.

name: Meda
preview: An Ashtavarga herb that nourishes the fatty tissues (Meda Dhatu) and acts as a general tonic.

name: Kakoli
preview: From the Ashtavarga group, Kakoli is a cooling, nourishing tonic used in rejuvenative and aphrodisiac formulas.

name: Dhamasa
preview: Fagonia cretica is a bitter, cooling herb used to purify blood and treat skin disorders and fevers.

name: Rohisha
preview: A type of lemongrass (Cymbopogon martinii), its oil is used for joint pain, fever, and as an insect repellent.

name: Suryavarta
preview: Gynandropsis gynandra is used for earaches, headaches, and digestive issues.

name: Gangeruki
preview: Grewia tenax is a cooling and strengthening herb, used to relieve burning sensation and thirst.

name: Kumbhika
preview: Pistia stratiotes is an aquatic plant used externally for skin diseases and swelling.

name: Sthuneyaka
preview: Taxus baccata (Yew) is used in respiratory conditions like cough and asthma.

name: Utingana
preview: Blepharis edulis seeds are a potent aphrodisiac and rejuvenative tonic.

name: Kumari
preview: Known as Aloe Vera, Kumari is a premier herb for skin, liver detoxification, and regulating menstruation.

name: Asthishrinkhala
preview: Also called Hadjod (Cissus quadrangularis), it is the primary Ayurvedic herb for healing bone fractures and strengthening joints.

name: Bharangi
preview: Clerodendrum serratum is a specific herb for respiratory disorders like asthma, chronic cough, and rhinitis.

name: Arka
preview: Calotropis gigantea is a toxic plant, but its purified latex and leaves are used for skin diseases and as a purgative.

name: Indravaruni
preview: Citrullus colocynthis is a very strong purgative, used for severe constipation, ascites, and joint pain.

name: Kadali
preview: The plantain or banana plant's stem and flower are used for their cooling and astringent properties, especially in digestive health.

name: Tintidika
preview: Tamarind is a sour fruit used to stimulate appetite and digestion, but can aggravate Pitta and Kapha.

name: Rudraksha
preview: Elaeocarpus ganitrus seeds are used to calm the mind, control high blood pressure, and for spiritual practices.

name: Putranjivaka
preview: Putranjiva roxburghii is renowned for its use in supporting female fertility and protecting pregnancy.

name: Padmaka
preview: Prunus cerasoides is a cooling herb used for skin diseases, burning sensations, and to stop bleeding.

name: Pilu
preview: The toothbrush tree (Salvadora persica) is used for oral hygiene, improving digestion, and reducing swelling.

name: Simsapa
preview: Dalbergia sissoo (Indian Rosewood) is used for skin diseases, obesity, and as a blood purifier.

name: Arishtaka
preview: Soapnut is used as a natural cleanser for hair and skin, and internally to induce vomiting.

name: Visha Tinduka
preview: Strychnos nux-vomica (Nux vomica) is a highly toxic herb used in purified, minute doses to stimulate the nervous system and digestion.

name: Devadali
preview: Luffa echinata is used to induce therapeutic nasal cleansing (Nasya) and vomiting (Vamana).

name: Dhava
preview: Anogeissus latifolia is an astringent herb used for wound healing, diarrhea, and bleeding disorders.

name: Gajapippali
preview: Scindapsus officinalis is a larger variety of pepper, used for respiratory and digestive issues.

name: Granthiparni
preview: Angelica glauca is an aromatic herb used for digestive, respiratory, and nervous system disorders.

name: Hapusa
preview: Juniper berries are a diuretic and antiseptic used for urinary tract infections and joint pain.

name: Kakajangha
preview: Peristrophe paniculata is used for its anti-inflammatory properties and in treating fever.

name: Karmaranga
preview: Star fruit is a sour fruit used to stimulate appetite and digestion, but can aggravate Pitta and Kapha.

name: Kasheruka
preview: Scirpus grossus is a cooling, diuretic, and aphrodisiac herb.

name: Kembuka
preview: Costus speciosus is used to treat respiratory conditions, skin diseases, and to purify the blood.

name: Koshamra
preview: Schleichera oleosa is used in traditional medicine for skin diseases and promoting hair growth.

name: Latakaranja
preview: Caesalpinia crista is a bitter tonic used for fever, malaria, and intestinal worms.

name: Lavali
preview: Cicca acida is a sour fruit that aids digestion and has a laxative effect.

name: Mahanimba
preview: Melia azedarach is a potent anti-microbial and blood purifier, similar to but stronger than Neem.

name: Nagabala
preview: Grewia hirsuta is a powerful tonic (rasayana) that enhances strength and calms the nervous system.

name: Pattanga
preview: Caesalpinia sappan (Sappanwood) is an astringent used to stop bleeding and heal wounds.

name: Sarja
preview: The resin of Vateria indica is used as an antiseptic, anti-inflammatory and for wound healing.

name: Somavalli
preview: The legendary Soma plant, often identified as Sarcostemma acidum, is a rejuvenative and vitality booster.

name: Vyaghranakhi
preview: Capparis zeylanica is used for its anti-inflammatory properties in conditions like edema and joint pain.

name: Akshotaka
preview: Walnut is a nourishing brain tonic that pacifies Vata dosha and enhances strength and vitality.

name: Chakshushya
preview: As its name suggests, Cassia absus is a specific herb for improving eyesight and treating eye disorders.

name: Dadrughna
preview: Cassia alata is a potent anti-fungal herb used topically for skin diseases, especially ringworm.

name: Bandhuka
preview: Pentapetes phoenicea flower is used for its cooling and soothing properties in Pitta conditions.

name: Bhutakeshi
preview: Selinum wallichianum is a nervine tonic used to calm the mind and treat neurological conditions.

name: Riddhi
preview: An Ashtavarga herb, Riddhi is a general tonic used to promote strength, longevity, and tissue nourishment.

name: Vriddhi
preview: Paired with Riddhi in the Ashtavarga group, Vriddhi also enhances vitality and nourishes the body's tissues.

name: Akarakarabha
preview: Anacyclus pyrethrum is a powerful aphrodisiac and nervine stimulant, known to enhance vitality.

name: Mahabala
preview: Sida rhombifolia, a variety of Bala, is a superior tonic for building strength and calming the nervous system.

name: Chirayata
preview: Swertia chirata is an extremely bitter herb used for chronic fevers, liver disorders, and skin diseases.

name: Jalapippali
preview: Phyla nodiflora is a cooling herb used for its diuretic and anti-inflammatory properties.

name: Kasamarda
preview: Cassia occidentalis is used to treat cough, skin diseases, and to purify the blood.

name: Bhumyamalaki
preview: Phyllanthus niruri is a famous herb for liver and kidney health, especially in treating jaundice and stones.

name: Dugdhika
preview: Euphorbia hirta is used to treat respiratory ailments like asthma and bronchitis, and also for intestinal worms.

name: Grinjana
preview: Carrot is a nutritious vegetable that improves eyesight, purifies blood, and pacifies Vata.

name: Ishabgula
preview: Psyllium Husk is a bulk-forming laxative used to relieve constipation and soothe the digestive tract.

name: Kalajaji
preview: Nigella sativa (Kalonji) is a potent stimulant for digestion and respiration, used to pacify vata and Kapha.

name: Karira
preview: Capparis decidua is used as a digestive stimulant and for joint pain.

name: Karpasa
preview: The Cotton plant's seeds and roots are used as a nervine tonic and to promote lactation.

name: Kataka
preview: Strychnos potatorum (Clearing Nut) is used for eye diseases and to purify water.

name: Kovidara
preview: Bauhinia variegata, related to Kanchanar, is used for its astringent and anti-inflammatory properties.

name: Madayantika
preview: Henna (Lawsonia inermis) is a cooling herb used externally for skin diseases and burning sensations.

name: Madhukarkati
preview: Pomelo (Citrus maxima) is a refreshing fruit that aids digestion and acts as a cardiotonic.

name: Mulaka
preview: Radish is a pungent vegetable that is light and heating, helping to clear sinuses and stimulate digestion.

name: Nimbu
preview: Lemon is a sour fruit that stimulates digestion, cleanses the system, and pacifies Vata dosha.

name: Palankya
preview: Spinach is an iron-rich leafy vegetable, but its heavy and cold nature can aggravate vata and Kapha if not cooked properly.

name: Panasa
preview: Jackfruit is a nourishing, strength-promoting fruit that is heavy to digest and pacifies Vata and Pitta.

name: Parnayavani
preview: Coleus amboinicus (Indian Borage) is an aromatic herb used for cough, cold, and digestive issues.

name: Priyala
preview: Buchanania lanzan (Chironji) nuts are aphrodisiac and highly nourishing, pacifying Vata and Pitta.

name: Rasna
preview: Pluchea lanceolata is one of the best herbs for pacifying Vata dosha, especially in treating arthritis and joint pain.

name: Satyanashi
preview: Argemone mexicana is a purgative and detoxifying herb used for skin diseases and constipation.

name: Shvet Musli
preview: Chlorophytum borivilianum is a celebrated aphrodisiac and rejuvenative herb, often called 'White Gold'.

name: Taruni
preview: Rose (Rosa centifolia) is a cooling and calming herb, excellent for pacifying Pitta, soothing the heart, and for skin health.

name: Vanapsa
preview: Viola odorata (Sweet Violet) is used for cough, sore throat, and fevers.

name: Vathada
preview: Almond is a highly nourishing nut that pacifies Vata, builds tissues, and supports brain health.

name: Vetasa
preview: Salix caprea is an anti-inflammatory and analgesic herb, used in treating pain and burning sensations.

name: Yuthika
preview: A species of Jasmine, Yuthika is a cooling herb used for skin diseases and pacifying Pitta.

name: Agastya
preview: Sesbania grandiflora flowers and leaves are used for their cooling properties and in treating night blindness.

name: Amra Haridra
preview: Mango Ginger (Curcuma amada) looks like ginger but smells like raw mango. It is a carminative and appetizer.

name: Chavika
preview: Piper chaba, a relative of Pippali, is a potent digestive stimulant used to treat indigestion and abdominal pain.

name: Raktamoola
preview: The red variety of Chitrak (Plumbago rosea) is a more potent digestive and metabolic stimulant.

name: Mudgaparni
preview: Vigna trilobata is a cooling, strengthening herb, considered a rejuvenative (rasayana) and aphrodisiac.

name: Mashaparni
preview: Teramnus labialis is a nutritive tonic used for building tissues, improving strength, and calming the nerves.

name: Nili
preview: Indigofera tinctoria (Indigo) is used for promoting hair growth, treating liver disorders, and as an anti-toxic.

name: Vrihi
preview: Rice is considered nourishing, strength-promoting, and easy to digest, forming the staple of an Ayurvedic diet.

name: Yavasa
preview: Alhagi camelorum is a cooling, astringent herb used for bleeding disorders and pacifying Pitta.

name: Utpala
preview: The Blue Lotus (Nymphaea nouchali) is a cooling, calming herb for the heart and mind, used to relieve burning sensations.

name: Shara
preview: Saccharum munja is a cooling diuretic, used in urinary tract disorders and to quench excessive thirst.

name: Shankhini
preview: Canscora decussata is a nervine tonic similar to Shankhpushpi, used for improving memory and intellect.

name: Shana
preview: Crotalaria juncea is used in Ayurveda for its astringent properties to manage bleeding disorders.

name: Shaka
preview: Teak (Tectona grandis) wood is used for its cooling and astringent properties, helpful in skin and bleeding disorders.

name: Renuka
preview: Vitex agnus-castus is renowned for its effect on female hormones, helping to regulate menstruation.

name: Prativisha
preview: Aconitum palmatum is a non-toxic variety of Aconite, used as an alternative to Ativisha for fever and digestion.

name: Prapunnada
preview: Cassia tora (Chakramarda) is a potent herb for treating skin diseases, especially fungal infections like ringworm.

name: Nadi Hingu
preview: Gardenia gummifera resin is used as an antispasmodic and for digestive complaints, similar to Hingu.

name: Lata Kasturi
preview: Abelmoschus moschatus seeds are an aphrodisiac and are used as a tonic for the urinary system.

name: Lakuca
preview: Artocarpus lakoocha is an astringent fruit used to improve digestion and act as a liver tonic.

name: Karnikara
preview: Pterospermum acerifolium is used for its anti-inflammatory and wound-healing properties.

name: Kankataka
preview: Flacourtia indica is used as a digestive stimulant and for treating cough and respiratory ailments.

name: Kalamba
preview: Ipomoea reptans is a leafy green that promotes lactation and acts as a general tonic.

name: Kakatikta
preview: Luffa amara, a wild variety of Luffa, is a potent liver stimulant and purgative.

name: Kadara
preview: Acacia suma, a variety of Khadira, is used for its potent effects on skin diseases.

name: Jalavetasa
preview: Salix tetrasperma is an anti-inflammatory and analgesic herb used for pain and swelling.

name: Hijal
preview: Barringtonia acutangula is an astringent used for treating diarrhea and bleeding disorders.

name: Hastikarna Palasha
preview: Leea macrophylla is used for its anti-inflammatory and diuretic properties, especially in filariasis.

name: Ambasthaki
preview: Hibiscus sabdariffa is used as a cooling diuretic and a heart-healthy tonic.

name: Kharbhuja
preview: Musk melon is a cooling, diuretic fruit that effectively pacifies Pitta and relieves fatigue.

name: Shrigataka
preview: Water Chestnut (Singhara) is a nourishing, cooling, and aphrodisiac food, useful in bleeding disorders.

name: Tala
preview: The Palmyra Palm fruit is a cooling, nourishing tonic that pacifies Pitta and enhances strength.

name: Tilaparni
preview: Gynandropsis pentaphylla is a stimulating herb used for digestive issues and to relieve pain.

name: Varshabhu
preview: Trianthema portulacastrum, a variety of Punarnava, is a potent diuretic and anti-inflammatory.

name: Godhuma
preview: Wheat is a nourishing, strengthening, and Vata-pacifying grain, but its heavy nature can increase Kapha.

name: Adhaki
preview: Pigeon Pea (Tur Dal) is a common lentil that is light to digest and pacifies Kapha and Pitta.

name: Bakula
preview: Mimusops elengi is an astringent herb, its bark and flowers are used for dental health and to stop bleeding.

name: Bhurja
preview: The Himalayan Birch tree's bark was used as paper and is medicinally used for its anti-toxic properties.

name: Bijapura
preview: Citron fruit is a variety of lemon used to stimulate digestion and as a heart tonic.

name: Matsyakshi
preview: Alternanthera sessilis is a cooling herb used as a diuretic and for skin diseases.

name: Mayurashikha
preview: Actiniopteris radiata is used in Ayurveda for its styptic (bleeding-stopping) and anti-diarrheal properties.

name: Nagadanti
preview: Heliotropium indicum is an anti-inflammatory and wound-healing herb used for skin ulcers and insect bites.

name: Nikochaka
preview: Pistachio nuts are nourishing and strength-promoting, helping to pacify vata dosha.

name: Rajamasha
preview: Kidney bean is heavy and difficult to digest, increasing Vata. It is generally not recommended in large quantities.

name: Sarpaksi
preview: Ophiorrhiza mungos is a traditional antidote for snake venom and is used to treat toxicity.

name: Satina
preview: Pea is a common vegetable that is cooling but can increase Vata due to its dry and light qualities.

name: Upodika
preview: Basella alba (Malabar Spinach) is a nourishing, demulcent green that soothes the digestive tract.

name: Saptala
preview: Acacia concinna (Shikakai) is a famous natural cleanser for hair, promoting growth and preventing dandruff.

name: Suranjana
preview: Colchicum luteum is a specific remedy for gout and rheumatic arthritis, but should be used with caution.

name: Tumburu
preview: Zanthoxylum armatum (Toothache Tree) is a pungent herb used for oral hygiene, digestive issues, and parasites.

name: Alarka
preview: Calotropis procera is a white-flowered variety of Arka, sharing its potent purgative and skin-healing properties.

name: Kusumbha
preview: Safflower seeds and oil are used for their laxative properties and in managing heart conditions, but are very heating.

name: Akhuparni
preview: Ipomoea reniformis is a diuretic and anti-inflammatory herb, used in traditional medicine for urinary disorders.

name: Putikaranja
preview: Caesalpinia bonduc is a bitter tonic used for fever, malaria, and intestinal worms, similar to Latakaranja.

name: Amlaparni
preview: Rheum emodi (Indian Rhubarb) is a potent purgative and is used for constipation and liver disorders.

name: Aranya Jeeraka
preview: Vernonia anthelmintica (Wild Cumin) is a powerful herb for destroying intestinal worms and parasites.

name: Bhadra
preview: Aerva lanata is a diuretic herb used for urinary stones and painful urination.

name: Bhumi Jambuka
preview: Premna herbacea is an anti-inflammatory herb used in rheumatic conditions.

name: Bhutika
preview: Lemongrass (Cymbopogon citratus) is an aromatic, carminative herb that relieves bloating and fever.

name: Chakragaja
preview: Celosia argentea is a diuretic and is used for treating urinary stones and painful urination.

name: Chhatraka
preview: Mushroom is a heavy, nourishing food that can increase Kapha and is difficult to digest for some.

name: Dhamargava
preview: Luffa cylindrica (Ridge Gourd) is a vegetable used for its detoxifying properties, especially for the liver.

name: Gavedhuka
preview: Coix lacryma-jobi (Job's Tears) is a diuretic and nourishing grain, used for edema and obesity.

name: Kshavaka
preview: Centipeda minima is a pungent herb used to induce sneezing and clear the nasal passages.

name: Kurabaka
preview: Barleria cristata is used for its anti-inflammatory properties in treating swelling and pain.

name: Mamsarohini
preview: Soymida febrifuga is a potent astringent used for wound healing and treating diarrhea.

name: Medasaka
preview: Litsea glutinosa is used as a demulcent and for its anti-inflammatory properties, especially in poultices.

name: Aivana
preview: Hyoscyamus niger (Henbane) is a toxic nervine sedative used in purified, minute doses for pain and convulsions.

name: Asphota
preview: A variety of Aparajita (Clitoria ternatea), sharing its brain-tonic and calming properties.

name: Abhishuka
preview: Abhishuka (Pistacia vera) is a highly nourishing nut used in Ayurveda to pacify Vata, restore body weight, and improve physical endurance.

name: Muranji
preview: Muranji (Moringa concanensis) is a potent wild relative of the drumstick tree used primarily for breaking down deep-seated swellings, abscesses, and clearing parasites.

name: Mayaphala
preview: Mayaphala (Quercus infectoria) is a premier classical astringent agent used to arrest bleeding, manage severe diarrhea, and tighten loose oral tissues.

name: Kankra
preview: Kankra (Bruguiera gymnorhiza) is a specialized coastal mangrove species whose astringent bark is traditionally utilized to cure stubborn diarrhea and process weeping wounds.

name: Sadapushpi
preview: Sadapushpi (Catharanthus roseus) is incorporated into modern Ayurvedic research protocols for its potent anti-diabetic activity and systemic alkaloid properties.

name: Sahachara
preview: Sahachara (Barleria prionitis) is a critical classical botanical targeting severe neurological and joint conditions, acting as the foundation for medicated oils treating lower limb weakness.
